title: Evangelion Laserdisc Release
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion Laserdisc Release...             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Fri May 17, 2013 4:04 pm Now from what I've heard, Neon Genesis Evangelion was released on Laserdisc as well as DVD. Laserdisc, to the best of my knowledge, has infinitely superior quality to VHS and slightly better quality than DVD, but has the disadvantage of being: A: Very rare, And B: Occasionally being botched (Star Wars Laserdisc releases for example). So, anyway, do the advantages of the Laserdisc release (Quality, "Ooh, Rare Format, Shiny!" factor, rarity+bragging rights, etc) outweigh the disadvantages (rairty+cost, difficulty of finding a Laserdisc player, ridiculous size)? Also, are they like DVDs in that you can watch them with subtitles or dubbing, or are they like VHS with only one voicetrack? Also, if you do recommend them where is the best place to find them? Thanks!

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri May 17, 2013 4:13 pm I have the first, still factory sealed. I got it for $40 through a specialty laserdisc retailer. While yes, the two US Eva laserdiscs are rare, there's virtually no demand, so what it's going to cost is really whatever someone wants, and whether you're willing to pay it.

[]:             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Fri May 17, 2013 4:15 pm Thanks! Are Laserdiscs region-locked?

[]:             Quote Post by Veefy » Sat May 18, 2013 10:03 pm Has someone done a good comparison between the laser disc versions and the dvd's. ie. a whole bunch of side by side comparison Picture quality screenshots? I'm not an expert but supposedly converting laserdiscs into other formats isn't too difficult. I don't think the format ever had copy protection. Though i have my doubts that if you looked for say a torrent of the laser disc converted versions you'd find an active one easily. Because I'm sure that someone has done one, but probably before the era of torrents.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat May 18, 2013 10:31 pm Squigsquasher wrote: Thanks! Are Laserdiscs region-locked?

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Sun May 19, 2013 1:06 am svenge is the resident video expert, he would have an idea about the whole video quality issue as well as versions of episodes are on the LDs and whatnot. His website also has all the info you need about the Eva video releases including LD. Opting to go for an older format that has been OOP for years seems to be asking for more trouble than it's worth and probably isn't as easy as doing the same thing with listening to LPs vs. CDs since the former are still in use. And yeah, from a collecting perspective it would be more for the sake of having Eva in one of its original release formats than anything else. I have vol. 8 only because I got it for $5 and have no way to watch it since I don't have a player. Same thing thing with all those Eva Sega Saturn games I also have, I don't have a means to play them.

[]:             Quote Post by Veefy » Sun May 19, 2013 3:18 am The Eva Monkey wrote: No, not to my knowledge. And I'm fairly sure that Renewal/Platinum would EASILY trump laserdisc regardless of whether you're talking about the Japanese release, or the two LDs that were released stateside..

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun May 19, 2013 11:12 am Veefy wrote: This was what I thought. Because logically if laserdisc quality was exceptionally better than any other formats than people would have gone to the effort to convert it and it would come up as topic of conversation more often.

[]: Re: Evangelion Laserdisc Release...             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sun May 19, 2013 12:49 pm Squigsquasher wrote: Laserdisc, to the best of my knowledge, has infinitely superior quality to VHS and slightly better quality than DVD

[]: Re: Evangelion Laserdisc Release...             Quote Post by Tribblepoo » Mon May 20, 2013 10:47 am Hyper Shinchan wrote: This is an old myth.

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Fri May 24, 2013 3:21 pm The only Evangelion LDs that are worth getting (in my opinion) are Genesis 0:X , the Theatrical Edition box set, Genesis 0:13 and 0:14 , and " Symphony of Evangelion " (which is outside this scope of this discussion). Genesis 0:X is one of only two ways to get the true OA versions of Episodes 21-24 (except for the "Second Impact Box" DVD set #2 ). As for the Theatrical Edition box set, it's most notable for having the original version of Evangelion:Rebirth (which was only available via Japanese VHS, LD, and pre-Renewal DVD releases; the US Manga release is actually a modified version of "Air") and the large amounts of merchandise included in the set. Genesis 0:13 and 0:14 have the modified " Genesis Version " of "Air" (0:13) and "Magokoro wo Kimi ni" (0:14), which can only be found there and on Volume 7 of the Japanese pre-Renewal DVD release. Regarding the two US Laserdiscs, they used the digital audio track for English dialogue, the analog audio track for Japanese dialogue, and closed-captioning for English subtitles to accompany the Japanese dialog. Note that the pre-Renewal DVDs for Evangelion used the exact same masters as the LD releases. That's why Volume 7 of the pre-Renewal DVD release had the program order of TV25 -> Air -> TV26 -> Magokoro wo Kimi ni, which is what would happen if you just did a straight dump of the finished LD masters of Genesis 0:13 and 0:14 back-to-back onto DVD.

Laserdisc, to the best of my knowledge, has infinitely superior quality to VHS and slightly better quality than DVD, but has the disadvantage of being: A: Very rare, And B: Occasionally being botched (Star Wars Laserdisc releases for example
